assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md contains a '## Repository Registry' section with a table listing both 'trustify-backend' (serena_backend) and 'trustify-ui' (serena_ui) as entries
assertion_results.evidence: Lines 20-25 contain '## Repository Registry' with a table. Row 1: 'trustify-backend | Rust backend service | serena_backend | /home/user/trustify-backend'. Row 2: 'trustify-ui | TypeScript frontend | serena_ui | /home/user/trustify-ui'. Both repositories with their Serena instances are present.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md contains a '## Jira Configuration' section with all 5 fields: Project key (TC), Cloud ID, Feature issue type ID (10142), Git Pull Request custom field (customfield_10875), GitHub Issue custom field (customfield_10747)
assertion_results.evidence: Lines 27-33 contain '## Jira Configuration' with all 5 fields: 'Project key: TC', 'Cloud ID: 2b9e35e3-6bd3-4cec-b838-f4249ee02432', 'Feature issue type ID: 10142', 'Git Pull Request custom field: customfield_10875', 'GitHub Issue custom field: customfield_10747'.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md contains a '## Code Intelligence' section that documents the mcp__<instance>__<tool> naming convention
assertion_results.evidence: Lines 35-37 contain '## Code Intelligence' followed by 'Tools are prefixed by Serena instance name: `mcp__<instance>__<tool>`.' The naming convention is clearly documented.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md contains a '### Limitations' subheading under Code Intelligence
assertion_results.evidence: Line 48 contains '### Limitations' which appears under the '## Code Intelligence' section (line 35). It lists limitations for both serena_backend and serena_ui instances on lines 50-51.

assertion_results.text: outputs/discovery-log.md mentions discovering 2 Serena instances (serena_backend and serena_ui) from the MCP tool listing
assertion_results.evidence: Line 5: 'Discovered 2 Serena instances from the simulated MCP tool listing:'. Line 7: '1. **serena_backend** -- identified by tools prefixed with `mcp__serena_backend__`'. Line 8: '2. **serena_ui** -- identified by tools prefixed with `mcp__serena_ui__`'.

assertion_results.text: outputs/changes-log.md indicates all Project Configuration sections (Repository Registry, Jira Configuration, Code Intelligence, Bug Configuration) were newly added — pre-existing non-configuration content from the CLAUDE.md (like the project heading and Documentation section) may appear as preserved, which is expected since only Project Configuration was missing
assertion_results.evidence: Lines 5-10 list all four sections as newly added: '1. **Repository Registry** -- new table', '2. **Jira Configuration** -- new section', '3. **Code Intelligence** -- new section', '4. **Bug Configuration** -- new section'. Lines 18-23 confirm pre-existing content was preserved: 'Project heading (`# my-project`)', 'Documentation section', 'Getting Started section'. Line 24: 'No existing content was modified or removed.'

assertion_results.text: The Repository Registry table contains columns: Repository, Role, Serena Instance, Path — matching project-config.template.md structure
assertion_results.evidence: Line 22 of claude-md-result.md: '| Repository | Role | Serena Instance | Path |'. All four required columns are present in the correct order.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md contains a '## Bug Configuration' section with three fields: Bug issue type ID (10001), Bug template (docs/bug-template.md), Bug-to-Task link type (Blocks)
assertion_results.evidence: Lines 53-57 contain '## Bug Configuration' with all three fields: 'Bug issue type ID: 10001', 'Bug template: docs/bug-template.md', 'Bug-to-Task link type: Blocks'.

assertion_results.text: The skill offers to enable security triage for the project (Step 9 opt-in prompt) and respects the user's decline — outputs/claude-md-result.md does NOT contain a '## Security Configuration' section
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-log.md lines 42-44 confirm the opt-in was offered and declined: 'User was offered the opt-in to enable security triage' and 'User declined -- no Security Configuration section added to CLAUDE.md'. claude-md-result.md does not contain any '## Security Configuration' section anywhere in the file (confirmed by reviewing all 58 lines).
